Faults Found ------------  * Logic board: Our diagnostic confirmed extensive liquid damage across the logic board, affecting multiple critical power and data circuits. This level of damage explains why the MacBook is not powering on. To restore stable operation, the affected areas will require full board-level rebuilding and thorough cleaning to remove all residue. * Charging ports: Liquid damage has affected both charging ports. Both will need to be replaced. * Touch ID: Touch ID is not working due to liquid damage affecting the sensor line. To fix the issue, we would need to order the replacement Touch ID from Apple, but this would arrive after the holidays. * Data status: The data is safe and accessible. If preferred, we can proceed with a data-only repair focused solely on data recovery rather than full functional restoration.  We can confirm that your MacBook is repairable, and your data is safe. Please see your options below.  Required Repairs ----------------  * MacBook Pro 14 M1 Pro/Max A2442 Tier 3 Logic Board Repair & Liquid Damage Treatment: £399 * MacBook Pro 14 M1 Pro/Max A2442 Charging Ports: £199 * MacBook Pro 14 M1 Pro/Max A2442 Touch ID: £199 Total: £797 inc VAT discounted to £648 (save £149)  Repair Details --------------  We can complete all repairs apart from the Touch ID by tomorrow (our last day before the holidays). To repair the Touch ID, we would need to order the required part once we’re back from the holidays on the 5th of January. The part typically arrives within 5 to 7 working days. In the meantime, we would temporarily disconnect the Touch ID from the device. This means the fingerprint sensor and the power button functionality would not work during that period, but the MacBook would remain fully usable otherwise.

@Safan Patel please complete logic board repair, liquid damage treatment and charging ports replacement. Also disconnect Touch ID. We will order a replacement one once we are back.    @Adil Azad just FYI, the client already knows that until the Touch ID is replaced she cannot use her fingerprint sensor or power button. Just please explain to her how she can switch off the device or turn it on if needed. Ideally, it should go in sleep mode for now

Faults Found ------------  * Logic board: Liquid ingress has affected the main logic board, impacting multiple critical circuits responsible for power distribution, data handling, and component communication. To restore stable and reliable operation, the logic board would require full replacement or extensive board-level rebuilding. * Screen: Liquid ingress has damaged the display, causing it to fail. The screen would need to be fully replaced to restore normal visual output and touch functionality. * Charging port: Liquid contamination has affected the charging port as well. A full charging port replacement would be required to restore reliable power input. * Rear cameras: Liquid ingress has reached the rear camera module, resulting in the component failure. The camera module would need to be replaced. * Face ID system: Liquid damage is present in the Face ID area, and it caused it to fail. To replace it, we would need to order the part directly from Apple. * Housing and internal areas: Liquid has spread throughout the internal housing, affecting multiple internal areas and increasing the risk of corrosion and long-term instability. The overall housing and internal structure would need addressing as part of any full functional repair.  Due to the extent and spread of the liquid damage across multiple critical components, the only feasible repair would be a data recovery. This would involve repairing the device enough to allow us to complete a full backup of your data to your iCloud account. We can confirm that the device contains approximately 480 GB of data, so a 2 TB iCloud plan would be required to ensure sufficient space (more on this here: https://www.apple.com/uk/icloud/). This process will need to be completed after we return from the holiday break, as it will take several days to carry out safely. Once completed, we will confirm as soon as the backup is finished so you can restore the data onto your replacement device.

Faults Found ------------  * Screen: The iPhone powers on, but the display is damaged and not usable. To fix this, the screen will need to be replaced. We can fit either a genuine Apple display or an aftermarket soft OLED. The genuine option offers stronger glass, better colour accuracy, higher brightness, and more responsive touch. The aftermarket screen works well for general use but has weaker glass, lower brightness, slightly reduced touch sensitivity, and less accurate colours. * Rear glass: The back glass is damaged. While the phone is currently powering on, broken rear glass can allow debris inside the device and may cause further issues over time. The rear glass replacement is optional but recommended.  We can confirm that your iPhone is repairable, please see your options below.  Required Repairs ----------------  * iPhone 15 Pro Max Screen: £359 inc VAT or  * iPhone 15 Pro Max Aftermarket Screen: £249 inc VAT  Optional Repairs ----------------  * iPhone 15 Pro Max Rear Glass: £179 Total (including Genuine Apple screen): £538 inc VAT discounted to £489 (save £49)  Total (including Aftermarket screen): £428 inc VAT discounted to £399 (save £29)  Repair Details --------------  Repairs will be completed within 1 working day once approved.

@Safan Patel please try the following passwords:  Olivia2019  Sweatshop2014  Mykonos2016  Mykonos2016!  Mykonos_2016  Sweatshop2014  Wallpaper88  Carlita88  Olivia2019Olivia  OliviaAndNico  Olivia2019_Nico2023    If the account starts getting locked let me know please

Faults Found ------------  * Logic board: Our diagnostic identified liquid damage on the logic board. We will need to complete our MacBook liquid damage treatment to properly clean, stabilise, and restore all the affected components. * Charging port: We also found clear liquid damage on the charging port, as shown in the image attached. Because the port has been affected by liquid ingress, it is no longer working and will need to be replaced to restore stable charging. * Battery: The battery has become faulty as a direct result of the liquid damage. The charging port and battery are connected through the same power distribution and charging circuits. When liquid entered this area, it damaged the Battery Management System (BMS), which is a small control board built into the battery itself. The BMS is responsible for allowing the battery to communicate with the logic board and report battery data correctly. Since this component is no longer functioning, the system cannot read the battery, and the battery will need to be replaced to restore normal operation.  We can confirm that your MacBook is repairable, please see your options below.  Required Repairs ----------------  * MacBook Pro 13 M2 A2338 Battery: £199 * MacBook Pro 13 M2 A2338 Charging Port: £159 * MacBook Liquid Damage Treatment: £149 Total: £507 inc VAT discounted to £429 (save £78)  Repair Details --------------  Repairs will be completed within 3 working days once approved.

@Mykhailo Kepeshchuk please proceed with diagnostic and let me know if you see further issues/liquid damage. If everything is fine and the client only needs the speaker, he has already agreed on the repair. Please notify me so I can send him the invoice.

Faults Found ------------  * Screen: The display is cracked around the area where the front camera cover sits. The cracking clearly originates from that point, which indicates pressure damage caused when the lid was closed with the camera cover in place. Unfortunately, these covers are known to apply uneven pressure on the LCD and often lead to this exact type of damage. For this reason, we would strongly recommend not using camera covers, as they can easily cause screen failure over time. * Logic board: During our inspection, we also found liquid damage on the logic board. This will require our full liquid damage treatment to properly clean and stabilise the affected areas and ensure reliable operation going forward. * Battery (optional): The battery health is currently reading at 82%. This does not indicate a fault at this stage, it simply shows normal wear over time. Once the battery health drops below 80%, macOS will begin to display a “Service Recommended” message, but for now the battery is still operating within expected limits.  We can confirm that your MacBook is repairable, please see your options below.  Required Repairs ----------------  * MacBook Pro 16 A2141 Screen: £499 * MacBook Liquid Damage Treatment: £99 Total: £598 inc VAT discounted to £499 (save £99)  Optional Repairs ----------------  * MacBook Pro 16 A2141 Battery: £199 New total: £698 inc VAT discounted to £599 (save £198 in total)  Repair Details --------------  Repairs will be completed within 3 working days once approved and paid.

------------ Faults Found ------------  * System / T2 firmware: When the device arrived, it was stuck in a state with no operating system loading. To stabilise the MacBook and proceed with diagnostics, we first restored the T2 security chip. This chip manages core firmware and security features such as Touch ID, Apple Wallet, and system integrity. When its firmware becomes corrupted, it can prevent macOS from loading correctly. Restoring the T2 resolved the software loop and allowed us to continue testing the hardware. * Touch Bar: Once the system was restored, we identified a fault with the Touch Bar. The Touch Bar itself is damaged and the display is no longer functioning. This is a hardware failure, and the Touch Bar will need to be replaced to restore normal operation. * Keyboard: We also found that the keyboard is sticky, which is consistent with liquid exposure. This indicates that liquid has affected the internal keyboard membrane. While the keyboard is currently still working, damage at this level often worsens over time and can lead to further failures. For this reason, we strongly recommend replacing the keyboard now, as any future issues caused by this damage would not be covered under warranty. * Battery: The battery health is currently reading at 80%. This is not a fault, but it does indicate normal wear and tear and that the battery is becoming depleted. Battery replacement is optional at this stage, but we do recommend it to maintain reliable performance and avoid unexpected shutdowns in the near future.  We can confirm that your MacBook is repairable, please see your options below.  ---------------- Required Repairs ----------------  * Touch Bar: £249 Total: £249 inc VAT  ---------------- Optional Repairs ----------------  * Battery: £199 * Keyboard: £249 Total (including battery): £448 inc VAT discounted to £399 (save £49)  Total (including keyboard): £498 inc VAT discounted to £429 (save £69)  Total (including both): £697 inc VAT discounted to £549 (save £148)  -------------- Repair Details --------------  Repairs will be completed within 2-3 working days once approved and paid.

CLIENT FORM RESPONSE SUMMARY  Issue Reported: Few days ago a little water spilled on it, i cleaned it out and the laptop was still working fine, no problem with the screen or anything, then i shut it down few minutes after only to try turning it back on 2 days later, it’s not turning on or even charging, i took it to a repair shop near me here in Manchester and paid for a liquid damage repair and the specialist said he has fixed the liquid damage but it’s still not coming on because it’s something related to the logic board that i should take it to apple, took it to apple and apple is saying it’s the logic board and calling an insane amount of money to fix it, then i made some research online and found you guys, even though it’s was far away in London i just trusted you will be able to help fix it for way lesser according to your videos i saw online. and that’s why i’m using you. Thanks  Additional Information: Diagnosed by Apple: Yes Device purchased: New Previous repairs: Not at all, this is the fix issue i am having with it, it’s a new macbook, got it late 2024, it’s not even up to a year and half Other issues noticed: Nothing at all, the device has been perfect Interested in battery replacement: No Data backed up: I don't know Passcode provided: Yes

------------ Faults Found ------------  * Logic board: Our diagnostic confirmed severe damage on the logic board, primarily caused by liquid ingress. Despite what was previously advised by the other repair shop, the liquid damage on the board has not been fully repaired and is still present. The most critical issue we identified is around the camera connector on the logic board. This connector is responsible for transferring signal data between the MacBook’s camera and the logic board. In this case, liquid damage and an electrical short in this area have caused the connector to fail.  In addition to the connector itself, the combination of liquid contamination and the resulting short has affected surrounding circuits and chips. These areas will need to be fully rebuilt at board level to ensure long-term reliability and prevent repeat failure. The camera connector will need to be replaced, and the damaged area beneath it will need to be properly treated and rebuilt. * Display: The camera connector runs between the logic board and the display assembly. Because the short originated on the logic board and travelled through the camera connection, the display-side circuitry has also been damaged. This has caused the display to fail completely. As a result, a full screen repair will be required in addition to the logic board work. We can confirm that the device is fully repairable. Your data has not been affected, and once the required repairs are completed, you will be able to continue using the MacBook normally.  ---------------- Required Repairs ----------------  * Tier 3 Logic Board Repair: £399 * Display Repair: £649 Total: £1,048 inc VAT discounted to £879 (save £169)  -------------- Repair Details --------------  Repairs will be completed within 2 working days once approved and paid.

------------ Faults Found ------------  * Logic board: Our diagnostic confirmed that the device has suffered liquid damage. The device is displaying a high-temperature warning because liquid has affected the logic board and caused the temperature sensor circuit to short. The liquid damage is located between the internal layers of the logic board, which means the board will need to be split, the layers cleaned thoroughly, and any damaged circuits repaired. * Charging port: The charging port is not working. We did not find direct evidence of liquid damage on the port itself, however it is likely that liquid damage on the logic board has affected the charging port flex line, leading to failure. * Battery: The battery is currently at 86% health. This is not considered faulty and is still operating normally. A genuine battery replacement would be optional. * Screen: We identified that the screen has been previously replaced with a non-original component. The display is currently working so replacement is not required at this stage, especially considering the cost (£339). Faults Found ------------  * Logic board: Our diagnostic confirmed extensive liquid damage across the logic board, affecting multiple critical power and data circuits. This level of damage explains why the MacBook is not powering on. To restore stable operation, the affected areas will require full board-level rebuilding and thorough cleaning to remove all residue. * Charging ports: Liquid damage has affected both charging ports. Both will need to be replaced. * Touch ID: Touch ID is not working due to liquid damage affecting the sensor line. To fix the issue, we would need to order the replacement Touch ID from Apple, but this would arrive after the holidays. * Data status: The data is safe and accessible. If preferred, we can proceed with a data-only repair focused solely on data recovery rather than full functional restoration.  We can confirm that your MacBook is repairable, and your data is safe. Please see your options below. To repair the Touch ID, we would need to order the required part once we’re back from the holidays on the 5th of January. The part typically arrives within 5 to 7 working days. In the meantime, we would temporarily disconnect the Touch ID from the device. This means the fingerprint sensor and the power button functionality would not work during that period, but the MacBook would remain fully usable otherwise.

customer_update.item_name: Update form to remove battery question
customer_update.text: Changes made:  * Removed battery question - Deleted the batteryInterest variable and removed it from the summary * Added data importance conditional field - Added handling for the new question "Is the data stored on your device important, and should it be preserved during the repair if possible?" which only appears when the backup answer is "No" or "I don't know" * Updated summary logic - The "Data important/preserve if possible" line only shows in the Monday update when the client answered No or "I don't know" to the backup question * Updated test pinData - Reflected the new form structure (removed battery question, added data importance field, updated passcode example) * Passcode question ("Are you able to provide a log-in passcode or password?"): Description changed to "To properly test your device before and after the repair, we would need temporary access. No personal data is accessed. If you’re happy to share the passcode, please type the passcode only in the field below. If you’d prefer not to share it, simply type NO."  The summary will now display like this:  * If client has data backed up: Shows "Data backed up: Yes" (no preservation question shown) * If client says No/I Don't Know: Shows "Data backed up: No" followed by "Data important/preserve if possible: Yes/No"

------------ Faults Found ------------  * Logic board: Our diagnostic confirmed a fault within the charging circuitry on the logic board. This circuit is responsible for regulating incoming power from the charging port and distributing it safely to the battery and the other areas of the device. When this section becomes shorted or unstable, the device will not charge correctly and will fail to power on. The affected charging circuit will need to be rebuilt to restore stable charging and startup functionality. This issue is fully repairable at board level.  We can confirm that your iPhone is repairable, and your data is safe. Please see your options below.  ---------------- Required Repairs ----------------  * iPhone 16 Pro Tier 2 Logic Board Repair: £299 Total: £299 inc VAT  -------------- Repair Details --------------  Repairs will be completed by tomorrow.

------------ Faults Found ------------  * Logic board: Our diagnostic identified extensive liquid damage on the logic board. The liquid ingress has affected multiple circuits, in particular the screen-related circuitry, including the backlight circuit and the image output circuit. This damage is the root cause of several of the symptoms observed on the device. * Screen: We confirmed that the liquid damage affecting the logic board caused a chain reaction that also damaged the display. The screen is electrically linked to the logic board through several circuits, including the backlight and image output circuits mentioned above. As a result of the failure on these lines, the circuitry within the screen itself has failed. Unfortunately, this type of damage is not repairable at component level and the display will need to be replaced. * Keyboard: We also identified an issue with the keyboard, where the key input feedback is inconsistent. This is caused by liquid ingress affecting the internal key network beneath the keyboard. Due to the same liquid damage, the keyboard backlight has also been affected and is no longer functioning correctly. Both the keyboard and the keyboard backlight will require replacement to restore proper operation.  We can confirm that your MacBook is repairable, and your data is safe. Please see your options below.  ---------------- Required Repairs ----------------  * Tier 2 Logic Board Repair: £249 * Keyboard & Keyboard Backlight Replacement: £289 * Genuine LCD Replacement: £599 Total: £1,137 inc VAT discounted to £899 (save £238)  -------------- Repair Details --------------  Repairs will be completed within 2-3 working days once approved.

------------ Faults Found ------------  * Logic board: Our diagnostic identified a fault within the main power management circuitry on the logic board. This circuit is responsible for distributing stable power from the battery to the other components during startup and normal operation. When this section becomes unstable or damaged, the device will not power on or respond to charging as expected. The fault was preventing proper power flow across the board, resulting in a complete no-power condition. The affected power circuitry will need to be rebuilt to restore stable and reliable operation. * Battery: We also identified that the battery is not detected by the device. The battery contains an internal management system that regulates charging, monitors health, and communicates with the logic board to ensure safe power delivery. When this system degrades or fails, the device may not detect it any longer. The battery will require replacement to ensure stable performance once the board-level fault is addressed.  We can confirm that your iPhone 12 is repairable, please see your options below.  ---------------- Required Repairs ----------------  * iPhone 12 Battery: £89 * iPhone 12 Logic Board Repair: £199 Total: £288 inc VAT discounted to £249 (save £39)  -------------- Repair Details --------------  Repairs will be completed within 2 working days once approved.

------------ Faults Found ------------  * Logic board: Our diagnostic identified a logic board level fault affecting the main power distribution line. This line is part of the MacBook’s primary power delivery system and is responsible for distributing regulated power to multiple subsystems during startup and normal operation. This rail is critical for initial power sequencing, and when it's compromised the system cannot correctly initialise its power stages, resulting in a no-power condition. We can confirm that this is a repairable logic board fault, and the affected power circuit will need to be rebuilt to restore stable power delivery and allow the device to power on normally. * Keyboard (optional): We also identified signs of liquid exposure affecting the keyboard assembly. The keyboard is currently functional, however, liquid residue within the keyboard layers can lead to corrosion over time and may cause intermittent or complete key failure in the future. For this reason, a keyboard replacement is recommended as a preventative repair to avoid potential long-term issues, though it is not required to restore the device to a full working condition.  We can confirm that your MacBook is repairable, and your data is safe. Please see your options below.  ---------------- Required Repairs ----------------  * Tier 3 Logic Board Repair: £349 Total: £349 inc VAT  ---------------- Optional Repairs ----------------  * Keyboard Replacement: £249 Total: £598 inc VAT discounted to £499 (save £99)  -------------- Repair Details --------------  Repairs will be completed within 2 working days once approved.

logistics_or_collection.item_name: Map out the In-Repair Status Updates + Fin AI Integration
logistics_or_collection.text: :clipboard: Repair Status Sync — Monday → Intercom → Finn  Objective: Enable Finn to auto-respond to customers chasing repair updates via a single repair_stage Intercom contact attribute synced from Monday.  Architecture: Monday status change (webhook) → Cloudflare Worker (conditional logic) → Intercom API (update contact attribute) → Finn checks attribute when customer messages  Step 1: Create "Under QC" Status on Monday Board: Main Board (349212843), Column: status4 Brief Ronnie: use "Under QC" instead of "Diagnostics" for QC.  Step 2: Create Intercom Contact Attribute Name: repair_stage | Type: String | Level: Contact  Step 3: Status Mapping (priority order, first match wins)  | # | Condition | Value | | --- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------- | | 1 | Status = Queued For Repair | `queued_for_repair` | | 2 | Status = Diagnostics, Repair Type = Diagnostic, Diag column empty | `in_diagnostic` | | 3 | Status = Repair Paused, Repair Type = Diagnostic, Diag column empty | `diagnostic_paused` | | 4 | Status = Diagnostic Complete | `awaiting_quote` | | 5 | Status = Under Repair/Under Refurb + (Diag column filled OR Repair Type = Repair) | `under_repair` | | 6 | Status = Repaired + Repair column filled | `awaiting_qc` | | 7 | Status = Under QC (new) | `awaiting_qc` | | 8 | Status = Password Req + Passcode empty | `awaiting_password` | | 9 | Status = Password Req + Passcode filled | `password_incorrect` | | 10 | Status = Awaiting Part | `awaiting_part` | | 11 | Status = Client To Contact | `client_contact_needed` | | 12 | Status = Book Courier + Received date empty | `awaiting_dispatch` | | 13 | Status = Book Return Courier + Received date filled | `awaiting_dispatch` | | 14 | Status = Ready To Collect | `ready_to_collect` | | 15 | Status = Received | `received` | | 16 | Status = Shipped/Returned | `dispatched` | Monday Column IDs: status4 (Status), status24 (Repair Type), multiple_person_mkwqj321 (Diagnostic), multiple_person_mkwqy930 (Repair), text8 (Passcode), date4 (Received Date)  Step 4: Finn Response Templates  • received → "We've received your device. It's in our intake queue and will be assigned shortly." • queued_for_repair → "Your device is in our repair queue. A technician will pick it up as soon as a workstation is available." • in_diagnostic → "Your device is currently being diagnosed. We'll update you once complete." • diagnostic_paused → "The diagnostic has been paused temporarily. We'll resume shortly." • awaiting_quote → "Diagnostic is complete. We're preparing a quote — you should receive it shortly." • under_repair → "Your device is currently being repaired by one of our specialists." • awaiting_qc → "Repair complete — currently undergoing quality control checks." • awaiting_password → "We need your device passcode to proceed. Could you please share it?" • password_incorrect → "The passcode provided doesn't seem to work. Could you double-check?" • awaiting_part → "We're waiting on a part to arrive. We'll update you when work resumes." [10:42 AM]• client_contact_needed → "Our team needs to discuss something about your repair. Someone will reach out shortly." • awaiting_dispatch → "Your device is ready — we're arranging the courier. Tracking details coming soon." • ready_to_collect → "Your repair is complete and ready for collection! Bring valid photo ID." • dispatched → "Your device has been dispatched and is on its way back to you." • Empty/not set → "Let me check on that — I'll get the team to look into your status." (escalate)  Implementation Checklist: ☐ Create "Under QC" status on Monday ☐ Brief Ronnie on new status ☐ Create repair_stage attribute in Intercom ☐ Build Cloudflare Worker with mapping logic ☐ Set up Monday webhook on status4 changes ☐ Test each mapping end-to-end ☐ Update Finn guidance doc ☐ Monitor first week  Full spec saved to docs. This does NOT affect existing automated emails (n8n workflow TDBSUDxpcW8e56y4).
